key_departures.impact: All-time franchise receiving leader departs after 12 seasons. Was limited to 8 games in 2025 due to injury, but remains a true WR1-caliber player. Chris Godwin, Emeka Egbuka, Jalen McMillan, and Tez Johnson remain, but none are proven replacements for Evans as a boundary X receiver.

Starting CB2 departs. Dean was a key part of the secondary and restructured his contract last offseason only to leave a year early. Zyon McCollum had a disappointing 2025 and hasn't locked down CB1. Tampa Bay's CB room now lacks proven depth behind McCollum.

2022 second-round pick who started all 17 games in 2025 departs. Never developed into an elite pass rusher but was a solid starter. His departure creates real depth issues on the interior DL alongside Vita Vea and an injury-prone Calijah Kancey.

Signed a 1yr/$14M deal in 2025 that massively underperformed. Finished with just 2.5 sacks in 13 games. Now a free agent again. His departure leaves EDGE as the team's most critical unresolved need — Yaya Diaby is the only proven presence on the edge.
Had already been displaced as RB1 by Bucky Irving in 2025 but was a versatile pass-catcher and secondary contributor. Departure is largely offset by Gainwell signing.
Backup QB who played in 4 games (including emergency start in Week 11) departs. Offset by Jake Browning signing.
Versatile DB and special teams contributor who could play safety and nickel departures. Partially offset by Miles Killebrew signing. Starters Antoine Winfield Jr. and Tykee Smith remain.
Rotational IDL departs. Compounds the loss of Logan Hall, increasing urgency on the interior DL.

14-year franchise LB deciding between return and retirement. Still led team with 114 tackles in 2025. His potential absence would be significant for the LB corps. ESPN reports a decision imminent. NOT yet confirmed as departed.

signings.analysis: Otton re-signs on a 3yr/$30M deal with $20M guaranteed — a real investment, not a bridge. He led all TEs in offensive snap % (87.5%) over the past 4 seasons. At $10M AAV, this is mid-tier starter money for the position. TE is fully addressed through at least 2028 with a 26-year-old starter in place.

Two-year, $17M deal brings in a proven starter who accumulated 490 tackles and 9.0 sacks over 5 years as Detroit's starting LB. At $8.5M AAV, this is mid-range starter money at LB. A real signing, not a bridge — but at 31, this is a 2-year fix, not a long-term solution. LB remains a secondary need, especially with Lavonte David's future uncertain. Does not fully solve the position.

1yr/$10M fully guaranteed — significant commitment for a one-year deal. Robinson (6'3", 320 lbs) provides the size Bowles wanted and posted 2.5 sacks and 65 tackles in 2025 with Carolina. He directly replaces Logan Hall's starter role alongside Vita Vea and gives the interior DL credible depth if Calijah Kancey (returning from pectoral IR) is healthy. However, this is still a 1-year deal at age 31, with the Panthers having just released him. IDL remains a draft-day consideration but is no longer a top-tier crisis need.

2yr/$14M directly replaces Rachaad White as the Bucky Irving complement. Gainwell rushed for 537 yards in Pittsburgh's committee and is a proven pass-catcher out of the backfield. With Tucker tendered for depth, Tampa's RB room is set. RB was never a top need — White's departure is cleanly replaced.

1yr/$6M (max value) for Muhammad, coming off a career year with 11 sacks, 53 pressures, and a 71.9 PFF pass-rush grade in Detroit. However, his previous season-high was 6.0 sacks (2021), making 2025 potentially an outlier season at age 31. This is a one-year, incentive-laden deal — clearly a prove-it structure, not a solution. He's projected as the No. 3 edge rusher behind Yaya Diaby. EDGE remains TB's biggest draft need. This move provides short-term depth but doesn't close the position need.

1-year deal (terms undisclosed) replaces Teddy Bridgewater as the backup behind Mayfield. Browning has starting experience with the Bengals and provides a credible option if Mayfield is injured. Procedural positional replacement — no need impact.

1yr/$1.8M. Two-time Pro Bowl special teamer (2023-24) who reunites with new ST coordinator Danny Smith from their Pittsburgh days. Killebrew is coming off a torn ACL (Week 6, 2025) and is projected to return by May-June. His role is primarily special teams, with limited defensive snaps. Partially offsets Christian Izien's departure but at a fraction of the defensive value. S as a position need is unchanged since Winfield and Tykee Smith remain the starters.
Low RFA tender keeps Tucker as RB3 depth behind Irving and Gainwell. Non-guaranteed, fully exposed. No material need impact — positional bookkeeping.
Exclusive rights re-sign at league minimum. Procedural move to retain developmental QB. No need impact.
Long snapper re-sign. Standard procedural move. No need impact.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Muhammad signing (1yr/$6M max, prove-it) does NOT address this need. He is a rotational/No. 3 EDGE at best. Yaya Diaby is the only bankable starter. TB had 37 sacks in 2025 — their lowest under Bowles. EDGE remains the single highest-priority draft need and should be Day 1 or 2 target.

Mike Evans departs to San Francisco (3yr/$42.4M). Godwin, Egbuka (returning from injury), McMillan, and Johnson remain, but none are proven WR1-caliber X receivers. Evans was TB's true boundary threat. Need created/escalated — WR2 or WR3 with upside should be a draft target in rounds 2-4.

Jamel Dean departs to Pittsburgh (3yr/$36.75M). Zyon McCollum (restructured) is CB1 by default after a disappointing 2025 season. No free agent CB signed to replace Dean. CB remains a critical need — draft target in rounds 1-3.

Robinson (1yr/$10M, fully gtd) partially offsets the loss of Hall and Gaines. Vita Vea (healthy) + Robinson + a returning Kancey (if healthy) gives the interior line functional depth. But Robinson is 31 on a 1-year deal and Kancey's health is a question mark. IDL drops from critical to a mid-round draft priority — likely rounds 3-5.

Anzalone (2yr/$17M) is a real starter acquisition and helps a depleted LB corps. However, Lavonte David's retirement/return decision is still pending. At 31, Anzalone is a 2-year bridge, not a long-term solution. If David retires, LB escalates back to tier 1. Even with both, depth is thin. LB holds as a rounds 2-4 draft consideration.
Otton re-signs on 3yr/$30M ($20M gtd) — a genuine starter commitment with a 26-year-old who has started every game he's appeared in. TE is resolved. No draft spend needed.
Gainwell (2yr/$14M) replaces White cleanly. Irving (RB1) + Gainwell (RB2) + Tucker (RB3, tendered) gives TB a complete backfield. RB need is resolved.

Winfield Jr. and Tykee Smith are the starters and both are retained. Killebrew (1yr/$1.8M) is a special-teamer who adds depth but not a defensive starter. Izien's departure leaves some nickel/depth hole but not a critical need. Draft in rounds 4-7 if value presents.

key_additions.headline: Franchise re-signs its starting TE through 2028; most snaps of any TE in the NFL over past 4 seasons
Proven 73-game Lions starter brings veteran leadership to TB's depleted linebacker corps
320-lb DT pairs with Vita Vea to give TB size-based interior run stuffing capability Todd Bowles demanded
Versatile pass-catching back replaces Rachaad White as Bucky Irving's complement in TB's backfield
11-sack pass rusher adds rotational EDGE depth on a prove-it deal; does not solve TB's starter need
Experienced Burrow backup replaces Bridgewater as Baker Mayfield's emergency option
Two-time Pro Bowl special teamer reunites with new ST coordinator Danny Smith to fix TB's struggling coverage units

key_losses.headline: Franchise all-time leading receiver and future Hall of Famer departs after 12 seasons; creates TB's biggest post-FA offensive void
Starting CB2 walks in free agency; TB has no proven replacement and CB becomes a urgent draft need
2022 2nd-round pick and 17-game starter departs; Robinson signing partially offsets but IDL depth remains thin
Disappointing 2.5-sack 2025 season ends his TB tenure; EDGE still TB's most critical unaddressed positional need
Former lead back departures cleanly replaced by Gainwell; limited need impact

pending_decisions.status: Decision imminent — return to TB or retirement per ESPN sources
pending_decisions.impact_if_gone: LB escalates from tier 2 to tier 1 need; Anzalone cannot anchor the position alone
pending_decisions.impact_if_returns: LB partially addressed; depth still needed in draft rounds 3-5

notes: Jake Browning contract financial terms not publicly disclosed as of 2026-03-13. Alex Anzalone guarantee breakdown not confirmed — $17M total reported by NFL.com/Spotrac but full guarantee split TBD. Muhammad deal reported as 'up to $6M' — base likely lower. Cap space post-FA is estimated; team restructured Goedeke and McCollum contracts to generate ~$26M in new 2026 cap room per Fox Sports reports.
